# Source Generators — Daily DevOps & .NET

> C# Source Generators in depth: how they hook the compiler, the build-time cost they add, and concrete techniques to measure and contain that overhead.

Source: https://daily-devops.net/tags/sourcegenerators/
Generated: 2026-07-22

## About this topic

- [Topic landing page](https://daily-devops.net/tags/sourcegenerators/): C# Source Generators in depth: how they hook the compiler, the build-time cost they add, and concrete techniques to measure and contain that overhead.

## Articles tagged "Source Generators" (3)

- [Incremental Source Generators Done Right: The Equality Contract](https://daily-devops.net/posts/incremental-source-generators-done-right/): Your incremental source generator rebuilds on every keystroke — because one record property silently broke the equality contract. Part 1 of 4: the mental model, why ImmutableArray betrays you, and the EquatableArray that fixes it.
- [TUnit.Mocks: No Castle, No Reflection, No Drama](https://daily-devops.net/posts/tunit-mocks-source-generated/): TUnit.Mocks brings source-generated, AOT-compatible mocks to .NET. No Castle.DynamicProxy, no runtime reflection. A practical comparison with NSubstitute.
- [Source Generators: The Build Performance Killer](https://daily-devops.net/posts/dotnet-source-generators-hidden-costs/): You added a NuGet package and your build jumped from 2 to 8 seconds. That package ships a source generator. Here is what it costs and how to find out.
